scope.summary: Feed exact per-job heartbeat observations into a durable CiLaneSnapshot through a bounded production poller while preserving unknown-on-missing-or-contradictory evidence.
out_of_scope: Changing which required-lane failures make the heartbeat workflow red.
Adding a paid always-on external polling service or a bespoke notification webhook.

entry_points.name: production CI lane poller
entry_points.trace: crates/oraclemcp-core/src/http/tests_ci_lanes.rs::production_poller_delivers_current_per_job_state_outside_the_request_path drives the production poller through a local HTTP server and proves the durable snapshot carries the exact job state.

entry_points.name: fail-closed polling failure path
entry_points.trace: crates/oraclemcp-core/src/http/tests_ci_lanes.rs::production_poller_persists_unknown_when_github_is_unavailable proves an unavailable API persists unknown rather than green.

entry_points.name: dashboard CI lane route
entry_points.trace: The isolated implementation verification ran the focused heartbeat suite 6/6, the production-poller tests 2/2, and the binary CI-lane tests 3/3; route tests prove stale, absent, and contradictory lanes render unknown.

entry_points.name: heartbeat workflow artifact
entry_points.trace: .github/workflows/ci-heartbeat.yml runs scripts/ci_heartbeat.sh and uploads its ci-heartbeat/v1 snapshot, while the service poller independently refreshes durable local state so dashboard delivery does not depend on copying that ephemeral artifact.

limitations: No live GitHub API poll is claimed by this close; production HTTP behavior and failure handling were exercised against a deterministic local HTTP server.
This close does not claim a required-proof/v1 artifact; readiness remains not-ready on scoped tests.
Scheduled and advisory lanes are visibility evidence and do not change the separate Required gate semantics.
